key_insight: The paper shows that adding retrieval, error tracing, and counterexample guidance can improve automated theorem proving for formally verifying consensus protocols in Isabelle.
engineering_relevance: This work points to a practical path for making formal verification of blockchain consensus systems more accessible, faster, and more scalable for real-world engineering use.
method: Builds on IsabeLLM, an AI-assisted theorem proving tool for Isabelle
Adds a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline to provide better context to the LLM
Implements error tracing, counterexample generation, and compatibility updates for the latest Isabelle and Sledgehammer
evidence_signal: The evaluation compares the original and improved IsabeLLM versions on completing the formal verification of Bitcoin's Proof of Work consensus.
watch_item: The approach depends on toolchain compatibility and the quality of retrieved or generated context, which may affect robustness across different proofs.
paper_title: IsabeLLM: Automated Theorem Proving Applied to Formally Verifying Consensus
